## Evidence at a glance {#sec-evidence .unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8). Reorganised by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5). Scaffold — the table will be generated from the model CSVs.
:::

{{< include _report_data.qmd >}}

_[Scaffold]_ A single table with **one row per outcome**, intended as the spine a reader can take in without the chapter sequence (and a seed for the plain-language publication). Columns:

| Outcome                | ITT effect $\tau$ (items, 95% CrI) | $P(\tau>0)$ | $P(\text{benefit} \ge \delta)$ | Crossover-model t2 contrast | Evidence label |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------ | --------------------------- | -------------- |
| Word reading           | _[from CSV]_                       |             |                                |                             |                |
| Letter–sound knowledge |                                    |             |                                |                             |                |
| …                      |                                    |             |                                |                             |                |

The table is auto-generated by a shared code chunk reading `rope_summary.csv` / `tau_summary.csv` / `did_summary.csv` across the ITT suite through the shared data-access helpers in `_report_data.qmd`, so the table and the per-chapter numbers cannot drift and **no value is hand-entered**. The crossover column uses `tau_t2`, the same randomised t2 arm comparison embedded in the longitudinal arm-by-wave model; agreement with the ITT is a parameterisation check on shared data, not independent evidence. Evidence labels qualify the _evidence_, never the effect size (@sec-bridge). Each cell is routed through `show_gated`, so a fit that fails the convergence gate shows a visible _REVIEW_ warning instead of its (uninterpretable) numbers, and until the suite is fitted each cell degrades to a visible _pending fit_ marker — for example:

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_gated("lrp-rli-itt-010", load_summary("lrp-rli-itt-010", "rope_summary"), "the word-reading ITT effect card")
```
